pageInventory.title: What Do Diabetes And The Dentist Have In Common? - OC Healthy Smiles
pageInventory.metaDesc: Most people don't normally associate dental issues with diabetes, but it is very common to see the early signs of diabetes at a routine oral exam. It is critical to detect diabetes early to reduce the risk of it developing into a more serious problem. This is just one of the many reasons to regularly
pageInventory.h1: What Do Diabetes And The Dentist Have In Common?
h3s: How Does Diabetes Affect Your Oral Health?
Does Poor Oral Hygiene Affect Diabetes?
What Are Some Signs That You May Have Diabetes?
Do I Tell My Dentist I Have Diabetes?
paragraphs: Posted March 21, 2018 in Uncategorized

Most people don’t normally associate dental issues with diabetes, but it is very common to see the early signs of diabetes at a routine oral exam. It is critical to detect diabetes early to reduce the risk of it developing into a more serious problem. This is just one of the many reasons to regularly see your dentist. In this article, we will touch on the association of diabetes at dental visits as well as going into how you can lessen your risk of diabetes.

Did you know that more than 8 million people are unaware that they have diabetes? This is a staggering number that can be reduced with routine dental visits. So how does diabetes affect your oral health? Everyone has glucose present in their saliva. When you’re diabetic and you do not regulate your glucose levels, high glucose levels cause harmful bacteria to grow. The bacteria mixes with the food particles stuck between your teeth to create a hard substance called plaque. The build-up of plaque can cause tooth decay, cavities, gum disease, and most commonly, bad breath.

A study by the CDC found that one in five cases of total tooth loss can be linked to diabetes. Serious complications that are associated with diabetes include heart disease, stroke, and kidney disease. If you are aware that you have diabetes, it is important to regulate your glucose levels and visit the dentist regularly to prevent tooth loss or other complications.

You may be thinking to yourself, “well I don’t have diabetes and my teeth look fine, so I don’t have to worry about my oral health.” Wrong! It has been found that poor oral hygiene, which causes serious gum disease, can affect blood glucose control and contribute to the progression of diabetes. Uncontrolled blood glucose levels can cause a person to become more susceptible to infections which makes it even harder to fight off gum disease.

pageInventory.h1: How Does Exercising Affect Your Oral Health?
h3s: Excercise Reduces The Risk of Periodontitis
Protecting Your Teeth While Working Out
paragraphs: Posted March 29, 2018 in Uncategorized

Have you ever wondered how exercising can affect your oral health? The answer may surprise you! Learn how to protect your smile from harmful habits that could make your workout detrimental to your oral health. Before exploring the drawback of working out, first, we will dig into the dental benefits.

The National Center for Biotechnology Information (NCBI) published a study in 2005 where they examined individuals who don’t smoke, formerly smoked, and currently smoked to see how exercise affected each of the groups. The finding from this 10-year study concluded that individuals who regularly exercise are less likely to have periodontal disease. They found that former smokers who exercised moderately 5 times a week or vigorously 3 times a week were 75% less likely to have periodontitis.

Periodontitis is the inflammation of the gum tissue and bone loss around your teeth and is commonly known as gum disease. Reducing the risk of this disease not only promotes a healthier mouth, it also lessens the risk of serious diseases like heart disease and diabetes.

Working out does help reduce the risk of periodontitis, but it may be surprising that working out is not 100% beneficial to your teeth. Some workout habits can contribute to tooth decay or even the growth of harmful bacteria. Read below to see how you can prevent the damaging effect of some workout habits.

pageInventory.title: What is Periodontal Disease? - OC Healthy Smiles
pageInventory.metaDesc: Commonly known as gum disease, periodontitis is the inflammation of the gum tissue and bone loss around your teeth. This is caused by a build-up of harmful bacteria in your mouth. Periodontal disease is very common, the CDC and ADA reported that 50% of the American population over 30 years of age has the disease.
paragraphs: Posted March 07, 2018 in Uncategorized

Commonly known as gum disease, periodontitis is the inflammation of the gum tissue and bone loss around your teeth. This is caused by a build-up of harmful bacteria in your mouth. Periodontal disease is very common, the CDC and ADA reported that 50% of the American population over 30 years of age has the disease.

Severe cases of periodontitis cause loss of teeth and risk of the infection spreading through your body. Periodontal disease develops quickly. If you experience any of the symptoms listed below, schedule an exam with your dentist as soon as possible.

Periodontitis starts out as gingivitis, which is the mildest form of gum disease and is mostly reversible. If you notice that your gums bleed when you brush your teeth or you have bad breath, this could be a sign of gingivitis.

Other signs of more advanced cases include, but are not limited to receding gum line, red, swollen, and tender gums, misaligned teeth, loose teeth, and pockets of pus between teeth and gums. These more advanced symptoms should be examined by your dentist for proper treatment. At OC Healthy Smiles, we offer appointment times Monday through Saturdays as early as 7 am and as late as 7 pm to ensure that you can receive treatment as soon as possible.

pageInventory.h1: What to Eat After Teeth Whitening
paragraphs: Posted February 26, 2018 in Uncategorized

All too often you hear the warning of what not to eat after teeth whitening but you are never told what you should eat. To make sure that your results last, you want to watch what you consume especially in the first 48 hours after your in-office whitening treatment.Foods that are mostly colorless are less likely to stain your teeth and are usually the best when keeping your smile brilliant. Listed are meal suggestions you can use for the first 48 hours after your whitening treatment to get long-lasting results.
Rather than skipping breakfast, try one of these meals after your whitening treatment. Even though your breakfast will lack color, it will be full of flavor!
· Coconut or plain yogurt with banana slices
· Rice cereal with milk

h3s: What to Bring on Your Date:
paragraphs: Posted February 12, 2018 in Uncategorized

Our OC Healthy Smiles dentists have put together a guide to help you look and feel your best on your Valentine’s Day date! These quick and easy steps will help you have the best oral health before, during, and after your date.
When thinking of getting up close and personal with your loved one on Valentine’s Day, you want to ensure that you give the best impression. Here are some great tips to for last minute preparations.

No one wants to go in for a Valentines Day smooch when your partner has bad breath. It is best to start off by thoroughly brushing your teeth and flossing. Flossing will help remove remaining bacteria and food particles out from between your teeth that could cause bad breath. Next, clean your tongue with a tongue scraper. Your tongue harbors bacteria which, if not properly cared for, can cause bad breath as well. If you are unable to brush your teeth right before going out, prepare yourself and your date by chewing sugar-free gum. The sugar-free gum will freshen your breath and help remove bacteria from your mouth. Lastly, stay hydrated! Be sure to drink plenty of water before and during your date to prevent dry mouth and bad breath.

Wow your partner with your brilliant white smile. If you have 90 minutes, you have enough time to get your smile up to 8 shades whiter with in-office whitening. If you have 5-7 days before your date, then custom whitening trays can be used to whiten your smile at home at your own leisure. If you choose the latter option, be sure to purchase whitening solution syringes to use with your trays. This solution is available to be purchased at OC Healthy Smiles without a prescription.

pageInventory.metaDesc: What is Oral Cancer? Cancer. The 6 letter word that no one wants to hear. Oral cancer is cancer that develops in any part of the mouth. It has one of the highest death rates of all cancers, not because it is hard to diagnose, but rather due to late detection. The early stages of
pageInventory.h1: World Cancer Day 2018: How to Prevent Oral Cancer
h3s: How Can I Detect It Early?
How Do I Schedule a Screening?
paragraphs: Posted February 04, 2018 in Uncategorized

Cancer. The 6 letter word that no one wants to hear. Oral cancer is cancer that develops in any part of the mouth. It has one of the highest death rates of all cancers, not because it is hard to diagnose, but rather due to late detection. The early stages of this cancer cause little to no discomfort. This attributes to its historically late discovery. In many cases, a physician or dentist can identify the early stages of oral cancer at a routine visit, the problem is, not enough people are going in for these routine visits. Every 60 minutes, someone dies due to oral cancer when a routine visit to the dentist could have saved their life.

Oral cancer no longer just effects male smokers, like research used to imply. Due to the complexities of the HPV virus, it is difficult if not impossible to define high-risk individuals. HPV (Human Papillomavirus) is a viral infection with over 100 different variations. Over 40 versions of HPV are passed through sexual contact that can lead to further problems in the mouth, genitals, or throat. An HPV Infection of the mouth can then lead to oral cancer. Because the cancer is fast moving and has very few symptoms, it is pertinent that you have an oral cancer screening bi-yearly, especially if you are sexually active.

Detection of oral cancer does not always happen in the dental office. When brushing, look for any lumps, or abnormalities in your mouth. The Oral Cancer Foundation has resources available on their website for purchase to perform an at-home basic check. If you notice that the abnormality doesn’t go away in two weeks, schedule an oral cancer screening with your dentist. Oral cancer can advance to an untreatable case in just 6 months. At OC Healthy Smiles, we offer an oral cancer screening at every visit. Our staff is trained to identify abnormalities during your visits, that you may have missed at an at-home check.

Studies show that about 15-25% of patients say that they don’t get an oral cancer screening at their dental visit. At OC Healthy Smiles, we understand the severity of oral cancer, and that is why all patients are screened at all their visit at with us. Our doctors perform a visual evaluation and have the equipment and technology to do a thorough oral cancer screening at every visit.

pageInventory.metaDesc: The winter Olympics are in full swing! These athletes have trained their whole life to have an opportunity to be in the Olympic games. Most athletes have had to deal with one or more major sports injuries. Whether it is a fall or slip, most of these Olympic athletes would not be standing where they
paragraphs: Posted February 21, 2018 in Uncategorized

The winter Olympics are in full swing! These athletes have trained their whole life to have an opportunity to be in the Olympic games. Most athletes have had to deal with one or more major sports injuries. Whether it is a fall or slip, most of these Olympic athletes would not be standing where they are now without their dentist. If you are an Olympic athlete or you just like to play recreational sports, I have some life and smile-saving advice for you.

The 16-year old Japanese athlete, Yuto Totsuka, took an extreme fall off the half pipe in the snowboarding competition last week. In the fall it looked like he went head first into the lip of the half pipe and continued to fall to the hard packed snow. Seeing this, it reminded me of the importance of safety gear such as mouth guards for athletes.

A custom-fitted mouthguard will do more than just protect your teeth. The soft plastic mouthguard will protect your gums, lips, cheeks, and head from injuries. Sports-related injuries such as head trauma can be greatly lessened with a custom-fitted mouthguard. A mouthguard is very valuable to all athletes and in many cases is required because it is a great preventative tool.

The Olympic committee understands the safety that mouthguards provide. For that reason, the committee has made it mandatory for all competing athletes have one. The Olympic committee spends time with athletes, trainers, and coaches reminding them of the benefits of mouthguards at all times. Whether you are an Olympic athlete or not, it is important to have this protection.

Luckily, dental offices like OC Healthy Smiles have realized this issue and are making efforts to reduce anxiety at dental visits. If your oral
pageInventory.h1: How To Cope With Dental Anxiety
paragraphs: Posted January 29, 2018 in Uncategorized

Dental anxiety is very real for over 30 million Americans who avoid the dentist due to fear. Pain is the number one contributor to this dental anxiety for patients over 25. Luckily, dental offices like OC Healthy Smiles have realized this issue and are making efforts to reduce anxiety at dental visits.
If your oral health is suffering due to dental anxieties, the following article will help you combat these fears at your next dental visit.

Recognizing you have dental fear is one thing, but dealing with it is a completely different affair. Do your best to pinpoint what causes the anxiety and explain it to your dentist. For example, if you are uncomfortable with the sounds associated with your treatment, let your dentist know. At OC Healthy Smiles, we offer headphones to all patients to help ease your mind during your visit. Options like headphones, Dish tv, and warm scented towels are great distractions your dentist can offer that will keep your mind from worrying.
If you have anxiety communicating with your dentist, we offer physical comfort menus at your visit where you can simply point to the aid you need.

pageInventory.metaDesc: Parents are often unsure of when to take their child to the dentist and have many other questions about their child's oral health. We put together a guide for parents on how you can best care for your child's oral health throughout their early years. What Age Should I Start Bringing My Child To The
pageInventory.h1: When Should I Start Taking My Child To The Dentist?
h3s: What Age Should I Start Bringing My Child To The Dentist?
How Should I Take Care Of My Infant’s Oral Health at Home?
When Should My Child Start Flossing?
Should My Child Use A Mouthguard?
Are Dental X-Rays Safe For My Child?
paragraphs: Posted December 04, 2017 in Uncategorized

Parents are often unsure of when to take their child to the dentist and have many other questions about their child’s oral health. We put together a guide for parents on how you can best care for your child’s oral health throughout their early years.

According to the American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry (AAPD), you should start bringing your child to the dentist regularly by your child’s first birthday or when their first tooth emerges. After their first visit, it is important to schedule your child for regular cleanings every 6 months in order to prevent cavities and other dental problems. Your child’s baby teeth will eventually fall out and be replaced by permanent teeth. Keeping the baby teeth clean and healthy will help your child chew, speak, and smile. The baby teeth hold the space in your child’s jaw for permanent teeth to grow in. If a baby tooth is neglected and falls out too early, it is possible for a permanent tooth to drift and cause crooked or crowded permanent teeth. By starting your infant off with proper oral care, you will protect their teeth for decades to come. We have a kind and gentle staff ready to meet all of you and your child’s dental needs at all your appointments at OC Healthy Smiles.

Long before their first tooth emerges – starting at birth, you should be assisting your child in effectively keeping their mouth and gums clean. Clean your infant’s gums daily with a soft infant toothbrush and water. Your child will enjoy the daily cleaning since they will likely be teething. Cleaning your child’s gums will soothe their sore and tender gums. Most children will not have their first teeth grow in until they are 6 months to 1 year old. When your child’s first tooth emerges, smear a tiny amount of toothpaste on an age-appropriate toothbrush. Ensure that your child spits out the toothpaste and does not swallow the excess after brushing. Your baby’s teeth should be cleaned twice daily. When your child becomes old enough to brush on their own, make sure to supervise and further assist with flossing. Start your child off with a lifetime of healthy habits by teaching them the importance of brushing and flossing their teeth.

You should start assisting your child with flossing as soon as they have two adjacent teeth. It is very common for children to get food particles and sticky candies stuck in between their teeth. Flossing these foods out will prevent bacteria build up and the formation of cavities. In most cases, brushing isn’t enough to get all food stuck between teeth and that is why flossing is so important for children and adults.

pageInventory.h1: 7 Dental Tips For A Healthier Smile
h3s: 4. See Your Dentist Prior To A Big Trip or Vacation
paragraphs: Posted January 08, 2018 in Uncategorized
Seven or more helpful tips to improve your oral health.
That’s right! Make your daily oral health routine count, by brushing and flossing properly.

Take the manual labor out of your daily routine and switch to an electric toothbrush. It has been proven that using an electric toothbrush removes more plaque and is more effective than a manual toothbrush.

Next time you go to the dentist and they ask you, “when was the last time you flossed?” be sure your answer isn’t 6 months ago at your last exam. Adding flossing to your daily routine will actually increase your life expectancy and reduce your risk of heart disease. When you floss, use a clean section of floss for each tooth so you do not reinsert the bacteria you just cleaned from a tooth.

It has been noted by the American Academy of Cosmetic Dentistry (AACD) that a smile is one of the first and most memorable features that people notice. To help you be successful in life, we have created
pageInventory.h1: How To Make A Great First Impression
h2s: How To Achieve Your Best Smile
How Do I Improve My Smile On A Budget
Tips On How To Achieve Your Best Profile Picture
paragraphs: Posted December 20, 2017 in Uncategorized

When seeking a professional or romantic relationship, it is important to make a memorable and positive first impression. It has been noted by the American Academy of Cosmetic Dentistry (AACD) that a smile is one of the first and most memorable features that people notice. To help you be successful in life, we have created a guide to transform your smile into one you can be proud of.

A study completed by Kelton Research found that people with crooked teeth are viewed as less intelligent than people with a straight smile. You may assume that metal braces are the only option to straighten your smile. Here is some great news! Advances in Invisalign clear braces are now technologically advanced enough to correct 95% of all crooked teeth cases. Invisalign corrects your smile with clear aligners rather than with metal brackets and wires. Choosing Invisalign over metal braces will not only boost your confidence, but it will also prevent you from getting poked in the cheek by wires every time you eat. The removable aligners allow you to brush your teeth efficient, easily removing food particles. You ’re ensured a bright smile without bad breath. When it comes to correcting your smile, there is not a one size fits all procedure. At OC Healthy Smiles, we will sit down with you and create a plan tailored to your dental wants and needs. Oftentimes, your doctor will recommend veneers when your teeth are not the size, shape, or color that you would want. Veneers are made of thin porcelain pieces that replicate your natural enamel. The porcelain is placed on your tooth, usually the front teeth, to achieve your desired smile.

A staggering 48% of adults believe that a smile is the most memorable aspect of an initial encounter. If you are on a budget, one of the most cost-effective and efficient options to start with is teeth whitening. Take home whitening trays made by your dentist will allow you to whiten your smile on your schedule. The whitening trays can be used for multiple applications if you take good care of them. Having your teeth whitened by your dentist during a 1-hour EZ-White session will help you achieve similar effects in a shorter amount of time.

You have the power to control your online image and perception. Many first encounters begin over the internet. A potential employer may look up your social media profile or a potential date may find you via your online dating profile. No matter what the avenue is, you want to ensure that your social media pictures are something you would be proud of them seeing. Match.com recently did a study on 5,500 singles and they found that the first thing that 37% of them noticed on a social media post was a smile. A tip when taking your profile or professional picture is to moisten your teeth and lips, this and wearing red lipstick actually brightens your smile. Using your custom whitening trays or having a 1-hour whitening treatment prior to having your photos taken will also greatly enhance your pictures.

pageInventory.title: Break Bad Habits, Not Teeth - OC Healthy Smiles
pageInventory.metaDesc: Our Costa Mesa dentists have compiled a list of some common bad habits and some suggestions on how you can break them. Brushing Too Hard Getting into the habit of brushing your teeth twice a day is one of the best habits you can start. On the other hand, if you brush too hard, you
paragraphs: Posted January 22, 2018 in Uncategorized
Our Costa Mesa dentists have compiled a list of some common bad habits and some suggestions on how you can break them.

Getting into the habit of brushing your teeth twice a day is one of the best habits you can start. On the other hand, if you brush too hard, you run the risk of irritating your gums and damaging your teeth. Continuously brushing hard could take off the enamel surrounding your teeth and expose your sensitive, yellowish dentin that is below.

While brushing is important, remember to massage your teeth rather than roughly scrubbing. According to the American Dental Association (ADA), you should use a small soft bristle toothbrush to help reduce hard brushing.

The habit of nail biting can lead you to chip a tooth or cause jaw pain. Nail-biting also introduces the bacteria and germs that are on your nails to your mouth. Chronic nail-biting can cause your teeth to shift and become crooked. This will lead to the need for correction with Invisalign.

On the other hand, if you brush too hard, you run the risk of irritating your gums and damaging your teeth. Continuously brushing hard could take off the enamel surrounding your teeth and expose your sensitive, yellowish dentin that is below. While brushing is important, remember to massage your teeth rather than roughly scrubbing. According to the American Dental Association (ADA), you should use a small soft bristle toothbrush to help reduce hard brushing. pageInventory.title: How To Care For Your Smile While Sick - OC Healthy Smiles
pageInventory.metaDesc: Here are a few tips on how to keep up your oral health while you are sick. Brush Your Teeth When feeling under the weather, it is important to take care of your body including your mouth. It is essential that you continue to brush your teeth even when you are not feeling your best.
pageInventory.h1: How To Care For Your Smile While Sick
paragraphs: Posted January 15, 2018 in Uncategorized
Here are a few tips on how to keep up your oral health while you are sick.

When feeling under the weather, it is important to take care of your body including your mouth. It is essential that you continue to brush your teeth even when you are not feeling your best. Brushing will prevent bacteria from staying in your mouth and decrease your risk for future dental issues. Remember to replace your toothbrush every 3-4 months and to let your brush air dry. There is no need to replace a new toothbrush after sickness since your chances of getting re-infected is very low.

Cough drops are the go-to over the counter treatment when fighting a sore throat. Sugar-free cough drops have little to no side effects and contain vitamins and/or herbs that can additionally aid a cold. Sucking on these will increase saliva flow in your mouth which is helpful in flushing out bacteria. Some cough-drops taste just like candy. That is because these options contain sugar as their main ingredient. Sugar-filled cough drops are just as bad as sucking on candy. Cavity-causing bacteria thrive under these conditions. When choosing cough drops, opt for a sugar-free option to best protect your smile from cavities.

Staying hydrated while you are sick is not only good for your body, it is also helpful for your smile. Some of the medicine that you are taking while sick may dry your mouth out. Drinking water rather than other sugar drink options is healthier for your whole body especially your teeth. Water flushes out food particles and quenches a dry mouth. Dry mouth can increase your risk of cavities. It is imperative that you continue to drink water.

pageInventory.title: What Are The Best And Worst Foods For Your Smile? - OC Healthy Smiles
pageInventory.metaDesc: Your mouth is the gateway to your body. As your dentist, it is our job to keep that gateway clean to ensure that you not only have a healthy smile, but also a healthy body. We have compiled a list of foods and beverages that you may consume daily that can help or hurt your
pageInventory.h1: What Are The Best And Worst Foods For Your Smile?
paragraphs: Posted November 29, 2017 in Uncategorized

Your mouth is the gateway to your body. As your dentist, it is our job to keep that gateway clean to ensure that you not only have a healthy smile, but also a healthy body. We have compiled a list of foods and beverages that you may consume daily that can help or hurt your smile.

Some of the usual culprits that affect the look and health of your smile include coffee, tea, soda, and red wine. According to the American Dental Association (ADA), coffees and teas can dry out your mouth and stain your teeth if consumed frequently. Soda and most carbonated drinks are very acidic, making them bad for your teeth since the acidity attacks and breaks down the enamel on your teeth. Red wine and alcohol reduce the saliva flow overtime which can lead to a multitude of dental problems. The most common problem from reduced saliva in the mouth is oral infections and gum disease. Red wine is known to stain your teeth if frequently consumed.

Here are some daily tips on lessening damage to your teeth. Use a straw to decrease the exposure of your teeth to any of these beverages. If you do drink coffee, tea or soda, drink plenty of water to help your mouth re-hydrate. Keep your coffee additives such as sugar and cream to a minimum.

Some foods are good for our body, but not for our teeth. If you know that a certain food or drink will stain your clothing, then it is most likely that it will also stain your teeth. Blueberries, pomegranates, and blackberries are full of healthy antioxidants but are a major contributor to staining your smile. White wine may not stain your teeth like red wine, but it does have properties to make the current stains on your teeth harder to get out.

pageInventory.metaDesc: One of the great things we do for our patients is provide a complimentary benefits check. While you are able to call your insurance company to obtain your dental benefit coverage, sometimes patients feel confused by the insurance company jargon. We are here to help and educate each and every one of our patients. One
pageInventory.h1: How Do I Check My Dental Insurance Benefits?
h3s: Finding Your Benefits Card And Plan Information
What Is The Difference Between PPO And HMO?
Will My Dental Exam And Cleaning Be Payed For?
Still Have Questions About Your Benefits?
What Will I Need For A Benefits Check?
paragraphs: Posted November 20, 2017 in Uncategorized

One of the great things we do for our patients is provide a complimentary benefits check. While you are able to call your insurance company to obtain your dental benefit coverage, sometimes patients feel confused by the insurance company jargon. We are here to help and educate each and every one of our patients. One of our goals at OC Healthy Smiles is to improve our patients’ lives through education, because in the end, it will help to improve your overall health.

First, find your dental insurance card for your plan. Make sure you are looking at your card for your dental insurance. Medical insurance cards usually don’t usually include dental benefits. Identify your insurance provider with your insurance card and visit your provider’s website. If the provider website allows, sign in to your account and view your current plan benefits. The provider site should have a variety of services to help you learn more about what they offer. If you don’t get enough information from the website, call your insurance and ask to speak to a representative. The representative should be able to let you know if your insurance is PPO, HMO, or a hybrid plan and what your insurance covers.

HMO plans require that you use a doctor who is contracted with the insurance company. PPOs typically allow you to use any doctor or provider. If an HMO patient chooses to go to a doctor that is out of network, it is likely that the insurance will not pay for this visit. OC Healthy Smiles does not want to compromise the quality of care of patients and for that reason, are considered out of network with HMO insurances. PPO plans allow you more flexibility when choosing a provider and specialists.

General exam and cleanings are considered “preventative” care by insurance companies. You will need to check the percentage that the insurance company will pay. If you see that your insurance will cover part of the fees, you would be responsible for the remaining percentage. For example, if it says “80% of preventative” is covered, then you would be responsible for 20% of the exam and cleaning fees.

pageInventory.metaDesc: A message from Dr. Mark Nguyen DDS: "At OC Healthy Smiles, we offer a technique called CT scan guided implant surgery. We are one one of the few offices in Orange County that has invested in this technology to safely and effectively to do implant surgery. Many patients are more satisfied with the results of
pageInventory.h1: What Is The Difference Between A Dental Implant Or A Bridge?
paragraphs: Posted November 22, 2017 in Uncategorized
A message from Dr. Mark Nguyen DDS:

“At OC Healthy Smiles, we offer a technique called CT scan guided implant surgery. We are one one of the few offices in Orange County that has invested in this technology to safely and effectively to do implant surgery. Many patients are more satisfied with the results of an implant rather than a bridge. Reasons to consider a dental implant over a bridge; a bridge requires a dentist to carve down some of the tooth structure on the two healthy teeth next to the missing tooth. An implant how ever, only requires work in the space where the missing tooth was. Another reason patient prefer implants is that a dental implant is much easier to keep clean than a bridge. And lastly, patients prefer implants because a dental implant is much more natural looking than a bridge.”

When replacing a missing tooth, it is likely that you will have to make the choice between a dental implant or a bridge. When faced with these options, it is always beneficial to be informed on some key facts about bridges and implants. The skilled dentists at OC Healthy Smiles, have been practicing for many years and use the most advanced technology for all of our patients. To make the process easier, we have put together some key information on both options so you can make an informed decision.

Bridges have been around for hundreds of years and have advanced, especially in the past 30 years. To replace the missing tooth, a bridge requires a dentist to carve down some of the tooth structure on the two healthy teeth next to the missing tooth. The teeth are then replaced with three or more connected crowns. The design of the crown effectively bridges the gap between teeth, but also makes brushing slightly more complicated. For good oral health, patients with bridges must use special brushing and flossing techniques to keep food particles and bacteria from being trapped. Depending on how well you take care of your bridge, they can last anywhere from 8 years to a lifetime.

pageInventory.metaDesc: Should I Have Dental X-Rays taken? What Are The Risks Of Dental X-Rays? Dental x-rays allow your doctor to identify decay in areas that may not be visible in an oral exam. These x-rays are key in maintaining proper oral health and can help your dentist identify dental problems in early stages that the naked
pageInventory.h1: Should I Have Dental X-Rays taken? What Are The Risks Of Dental X-Rays?
h3s: Should I Have Dental X-Rays taken? What Are The Risks Of Dental X-Rays?
I Do Not Want My Loved Ones To Be Exposed To Too Much Radiation. How Do I Lower The Radiation Effect, And Maintain Good Oral Health?
How Often Should I Have an X-Ray of My Teeth?
paragraphs: Posted November 13, 2017 in Uncategorized

Dental x-rays allow your doctor to identify decay in areas that may not be visible in an oral exam. These x-rays are key in maintaining proper oral health and can help your dentist identify dental problems in early stages that the naked eye cannot detect. Identifying problems early can potentially save you money and pain in the long run and is why getting dental x-rays are very important.

Having your dentist take an x-ray yearly will allow them to keep a record of your oral health and will give them a baseline to compare results to. Other problems that can be identified with x-rays include, but are not limited to, decay, bone loss, gum disease, infection, abscesses, cysts, wisdom teeth, and tumors.

Digital x-rays are offered at OC Healthy Smiles, and are considered safer than film x-rays because they have a lower exposure to radiation than film x-rays. The American Society of Radiologic Technologists reports that a set of standard digital x-rays at your dentist is one of the lowest radiation dose tests they have preformed. They report that it is the same amount of radiation that you would experience when aboard a short flight. The protective radiation equipment that dentist uses to shield the rays also lowers your exposure to these rays exponentially.

The timing of when you should have an x-ray done on your teeth is dependent on a couple of key factors. For example, If you are currently seeing your dentist and have a history or risk of gum disease, your dentist may elect to have x-rays taken as often as every 6 months.

When thinking of planning a wedding their are many things that come to mind, but have you thought of giving your dentist a call? Looking your best on your big day is more that just finding the perfect dress, it's a
pageInventory.h1: I Just Got Engaged, Now What?
paragraphs: Posted November 15, 2017 in Uncategorized

Congratulations! You just got engaged and you are going to marry that special someone! When thinking of planning a wedding their are many things that come to mind, but have you thought of giving your dentist a call?

Looking your best on your big day is more that just finding the perfect dress, it’s a feeling. When you feel your best, you look your best. Before your schedule becomes packed with cake tastings, venue visits, and honeymoon planning, make sure you make an appointment with your dentist.

You want the perfect smile for your wedding day, and if you start early, you can achieve that! If your teeth are not aligned correctly or just don’t look as good as you want, ask your dentist about Invisalign and Veneers.

Invisalign is the clear alternative to braces and it will straighten your smile without the hassle of metal brackets or brace face. Invisalign uses a series of clear trays to re-align your smile. With new technological advances, Invisalign is now able to correct a majority of cases from mild up to complex with the new SmartTrack technology. If you want you teeth straighten with Invisalign, but only have a few months before your wedding, ask your Dentist if Propel is right for you. Propel safely speeds up your treatment time 50% faster and goes hand-in-hand with Invisalign.

h3s: If you can’t get to the dentist immediately, here are some tips on how to relieve the pain until you can be seen
When do I go to the ER?
paragraphs: Posted November 01, 2017 in Uncategorized

pageInventory.h1: My Temporary Crown Fell Out – What do I do?
paragraphs: Posted November 06, 2017 in Uncategorized

Do you have a temporary crown or cap on and did it come off? Great news is that this can usually be fixed without an additional dental visit. Visit a local pharmacy and purchase dental adhesive to reattach the temporary. Make sure to contact you dentist and inform them that it fell off since they may want you to come in sooner. Try not to eat on the side of your mouth that the temporary is located, this will help prevent the crown from coming off again.
One benefits of coming to OC Healthy Smiles is same-day CEREC crowns. This means that you don’t have to worry about a temporary falling out or an extra dental visit to have a temporary replaced.
Call (714) 422-0596 to schedule your next exam with our highly skilled dentists.

pageInventory.h1: Sleep Apnea and Your Orange County, CA, Dentist
h3s: How Do I Know If I Have Sleep Apnea?
paragraphs: Posted January 10, 2017 in Uncategorized

In our Costa Mesa, CA, dentists’ office, we believe strongly in taking a comprehensive approach to dentistry. After all, maintaining your good health involves a lot more than just your teeth. One major health concern that some people deal with is sleep apnea, and what many don’t realize is that your Orange County, CA, dentist may actually be able to help you with this potentially life-threatening condition!

Sleep apnea is a disorder that affect you while you are sleeping, and it is marked by one or more pauses in breathing throughout the night. When you stop breathing in your sleep, your body and brain begins to be deprived of oxygen. Eventually, your body realizes that a potential emergency is about to happen, so it jump starts you into breathing again – often with a sudden, sharp intake of breath. If you have ever woken up coughing or gasping, that could certainly have been the result of sleep apnea.
If you show any of the following signs or symptoms, then you should speak with your health care provider about being evaluated for sleep apnea.
In addition, your sleep partner, if you have one, may observe one or more of the following while you are asleep:

pageInventory.h1: Don’t Let Gum Disease Affect Your Healthy Smile
h3s: Gingivitis: The Beginning Signs of Gum Disease
paragraphs: Posted January 10, 2017 in Uncategorized

The majority of tooth loss experienced by Americans today is the direct result of undiagnosed or untreated gum disease. This is particularly unfortunate, because gum disease is a completely preventable condition! Our top-rated dentists at OC Healthy Smiles in Costa Mesa, CA, are here to educate you on the causes and signs of gum disease, aka periodontal disease. With a little extra care, you can keep your teeth and gums healthy and intact.

Gingivitis, the early stage of periodontal disease, is your body’s natural response to being attacked by the bacteria contained in plaque. Gingivitis is usually identified through the following symptoms:

Sometimes, gingivitis can be present without you even realize it. As long as you see your dentist in Orange County, CA, for a dental cleaning and exam once every six months, your dentist will be able to identify whether you have gingivitis or not.

Gingivitis can be exacerbated if you don’t regularly brush and floss, if you use tobacco or cigarettes, and if you aren’t eating a proper, nutritional diet. Gingivitis can also be made more likely by genetics or hormone changes – in fact, many pregnant women find themselves suffering from gingivitis because of the influx of pregnancy hormones!

pageInventory.h1: Your Healthy Mouth – The Importance of Oral Health Care at Home
h3s: Tip #4: Ask Your Costa Mesa, CA, Dentist about Sealants
paragraphs: Posted January 10, 2017 in Uncategorized

You trust the health of your teeth and gums to your Costa Mesa, CA, dentist, but who’s in charge of keeping your mouth healthy in between your 6-month dental checkups? YOU, that’s who! Our OC Healthy Smiles dentists are here to give you a few tips on how to make sure your teeth stay in tip-top shape during the months between your regular dental cleanings in Orange County, CA.

When you indulge in sugar-heavy treats such as candy or soda, the sugary residue left behind on your teeth creates a breeding ground for unhealthy bacteria. Given enough time, that bacteria can result in a variety of problems, including tooth decay, cavities, and gum disease. To avoid getting “sugar bugs” in your mouth, limit your intake of high-sugar and high-corn-syrup foods and drinks. If you do have a sugary treat, then brush your teeth afterward – or at least rinse your mouth with plain water to dilute the sugar.

You’ve probably heard this one before, but it certainly bears repeating. Brushing at least twice a day and flossing at least once a day not only removes the sugar and food particles that attract bacteria; it also removes plaque before it can harden into tartar. Tartar can form in under 24 hours, so missing even one day of brushing means you will likely end up with tartar that your tooth brush won’t be able to remove.

Fluoride is a mineral that can actually repair very early damage to your teeth on a microscopic level. By drinking fluoride-infused water (such as city tap water) and by rinsing your mouth once a day with a fluoride-based mouthwash, you can help to keep your teeth strong and less prone to chipping or cracks.

pageInventory.h1: You Lost A Tooth Filling – Don’t Panic! By Your Top-Rated Costa Mesa, CA, Dentist
h3s: What Can Make A Tooth Filling Fall Out?
My Filling Fell Out! What Do I Do?
paragraphs: Posted January 09, 2017 in Uncategorized

If a tooth filling falls out, it leaves behind an open hole in your tooth that can be both uncomfortable and nerve wracking. You don’t need to panic, though, because losing a dental filling is actually one of the more common dental emergencies. That means your dentist should be able to get you fixed up and back to your regular routine in no time.

A tooth filling is exactly what the name sounds like: a special material that fills in a hole in your tooth. When you have tooth decay, your Orange County, CA, dentist cleans out the damaged part of your tooth before it can spread to the other, healthy parts of your tooth. Then your dentist fills the spot where the decay used to be with your dental filling. There are a range of options for filling material, including gold, porcelain, the old-school silver amalgam (made up of mercury mixed with other materials) that so many of us have in the backs of our mouths, and the increasingly popular tooth-colored composite resin.

The material that makes up a tooth filling is strong, but that doesn’t mean it lasts forever. Dental fillings can get worn down from all the chewing you do over the years, and so they eventually need to be replaced. Usually, your Orange County, CA, dentist will identify worn fillings during your regular dental cleaning and exam, so the filling gets replaced before it gets a chance to fall out. Nevertheless, there are a variety of factors that can cause fillings to fall out unexpectedly, for example:

As we’ve already said, don’t panic! Wash your hands, and then carefully check your mouth to remove the missing filling, as well as any pieces of broken tooth that may be there. Then call your Costa Mesa, CA, dental office right away. Tell the receptionist that you’ve lost a filling and need an emergency appointment. Most dentists keep emergency time slots available in their schedules for cases like this, because they know that you shouldn’t leave the sensitive underlying area of your tooth exposed. It’s important that you get your missing filling taken care of by a professional as quickly as possible.

pageInventory.h1: Valentine’s Smile Makeovers in Costa Mesa, CA
h3s: Goodbye Stains! Hello Professional Teeth Whitening!
Get in Shape with Dental Bonding
Restoring Smiles with Dental Implants or Dentures
paragraphs: Posted January 10, 2017 in Uncategorized

With many of the winter holidays already behind us, there is still one major one to come: Valentine’s Day! Whether you love it or hate it, Valentine’s Day can be a great opportunity to give yourself or your loved one a truly unique gift and a big real to smile brightly: a complete smile makeover!

At OC Healthy Smiles in Orange County, CA, we have been straightening, whitening, and improving smiles for years. A smile makeover can often be far simpler than many people even realize! For example, do you or your valentine deal with any of the following?

Any of these types of problems can be addressed with a smile makeover from your local Orange County, CA, dental office. For example, at our OC Healthy Smiles office in Costa Mesa, CA, we offer a wide variety of smile solutions, such as teeth whitening, dental bonding, dental implants, and dentures. Here is a crash course in what each of these procedures is and what it can do for your loved one’s smile.

Unlike with over-the-counter teeth whitening kits, a professional teeth whitening treatment from your Costa Mesa, CA, dentist can get a smile brighter in less time – AND with fewer risks! When not performed properly, a teeth whitening treatment can result in weakened tooth enamel, permanent tooth sensitivity, gum irritation, or other problems. When overseen by your dentist, however, you can be sure that the chemical concentration of the whitener is safe for your individual needs.
